mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% represents restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and credit costs, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirms broad-based demand strength that outpaces supply constraints, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of coincident months, or an F-test shows the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over main effects (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side overheating that prompts additional Fed hawkishness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as terminal rate expectations re-price higher.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO accelerates during periods of FEDFUNDS above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Falling CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) reduces nominal discount rate uncertainty and supports real earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,000,000 units signal robust residential investment and downstream consumption of durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when HOUST exceeds 1,000,000 units.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment (UNRATE) and rise in payrolls (PAYEMS) confirms labor market tightening without measurement noise, accelerating wage-driven consumption and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with both falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS fail to deliver positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate demand expansion that boosts expected corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 increases quarter-over-quarter.
